front: Novel: the Kid joins the Glanton Gang under Judge Holden, a giant 7-foot hairless polymath who claims 'Whatever in creation exists without my knowledge exists without my consent' and 'War is God'; dances naked with strippers after presumably murdering the Kid in an outhouse

Novel: the Kid is born in Tennessee during a meteor shower; first meets the Judge when the Judge crashes a religious revival accusing the preacher of bestiality; joins Captain White's filibustering militia before the Glanton Gang
Novel: John Grady Cole runs away to Mexico from his dead grandfather's ranch with friend Rawlins; has affair with Alejandra, whose father betrays them to the authorities
Novel: teenager Jimmy Blevins claims to be deaf in one ear after being struck by lightning; kills a man retrieving his Colt pistol and is eventually hanged
Novel: protagonist stabs a cuchillero to death in a prison knife fight after discussing countries with Perez; cauterizes a gunshot wound with a heated pistol barrel
Novel: Anton Chigurh pursues Llewelyn Moss who found $2.4M in drug money near the Mexican border; Chigurh carries a captive bolt stunner, flips coins to decide victims' fates, kills Carla Jean
back: No Country for Old Men (Cormac McCarthy)

front: Novel: title from Yeats's 'Sailing to Byzantium'; Coen brothers adapted it with Javier Bardem as Chigurh
back: No Country for Old Men (Cormac McCarthy)

front: Novel: ends with Sheriff Bell's dream of his dead father carrying a moon-colored horn lit with fire
back: No Country for Old Men (Cormac McCarthy)

front: Novel: unnamed father and son travel post-apocalyptic America; father tells son they are 'carrying the fire' and are 'good guys'; father has two bullets; ends with brook trout whose backs are 'maps of the world in its becoming'
Novel: protagonists find human infants cooked on a spit; a wall of human heads; the mother earlier killed herself with a piece of obsidian
Novel: won the Pulitzer Prize; inspired by McCarthy seeing 'fires on the hill' while visiting El Paso with his son
Novel: Cornelius Suttree abandons his luxurious life to live as a catfish-seller on a houseboat in Knoxville's McAnally Flats on the Tennessee River
Novel: Gene Harrogate ('the Moonlight Melonmounter') sent to a work camp for violating watermelons; befriends the title character
Novel: serial killer 'much like yourself perhaps' named Lester Ballard lives in a cave in Tennessee keeping bodies of murdered women; his body is dissected by medical students at the end
Novel: three unnamed characters burn a child in the same site where they hang a character known as the Tinker; sibling protagonists Culla and Rinthy
Novel: 2022 companion novels about mathematical prodigies whose father worked on the atomic bomb; sister hallucinates the Thalidomide Kid; Stella Maris is a 'coda' to The Passenger
